item.title: Topolski's Chronicle, V.4 No.1-2, 1956
item.creator: Topolski, Feliks, 1907-1989
item.description: Topolski's Chronicle is a series of broadsheets created by Feliks Topolski and distributed bimonthly to subscribers from 1953 through 1982. Topolski was a superlative draughtsman and covered current events with an artistic, unique style.

pages.typeset_text:
TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE No 1-2 (61-62) Vol IV 1956
of a voyage round the world (2)
ENGLAND
sketch_descriptions: Two stylized and loosely sketched standing figures seen from behind, outlined in black with red and beige coloring. One figure on the left has a tall, rounded hairstyle or headgear and a braided detail on the back; the other figure on the right wears a hat and leans slightly forward, holding a cane or stick.
An abstract sketch outline of a bird or similar creature drawn above the figures on the right side.
pages.page_summary: This page from Topolski's Chronicle (vol. IV, 1956) features abstract sketches of two figures in fashionable attire, labeled as England, as part of the artist's illustrated chronicle of a voyage around the world.

pages.typeset_text:
PARLIAMENT
LEARNING
LAW
CHURCH
PASTIME
FINANCE
FREE SPEECH
"TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE" IS PUBLISHED 24 TIMES A YEAR. AT 6d. A COPY. THIS DOUBLE ISSUE COSTS 1/-.
YEARLY SUBSCRIPTION RATES WITH DOUGLAS-AND-NICOL TO ANY WORLD ADDRESS:
  U.K., S.;  EUROPE & N. AMERICA, 8s. 6d.;  SOUTH AFRICA, 10s.;  FAR EAST, 12s. 6d.
SUBSCRIBERS TEND TO CONTINUE FOR YEARS.
pages.handwritten_text:
H. Topolski
Grecian in modern Oxford
Lewis Assizes
Pub in Summerset
Entrance to the Stock Exchange
Lord Jesus Christ
Morpeth speaker
sketch_descriptions: A loose pen sketch of several figures labeled 'PARLIAMENT' showing tall, somewhat elongated parliamentary figures in robes.
An intricate grouping of various individuals engaged in a learning or lecture setting, including a bust sculpture and a person reading, labeled 'LEARNING'.
A darker, more heavily shaded drawing of several people, mostly elderly, gathered formally labeled 'LAW', suggesting a courtroom or judges panel.
A dramatic, shadowy single figure in a cloak and hat labeled 'CHURCH', likely representing a clergy member or bishop.
A busy interior scene with several people socializing and drinking at a pub, labeled 'PASTIME', with a pub window and some bottles visible.
A sketch of a tall doorway or entrance with several well-dressed men in top hats outside, suggesting a formal financial institution, labeled 'FINANCE'.
A crowded outdoor scene featuring many figures listening to a speaker at a podium, with signs including 'LORD JESUS CHRIST' and 'Morpeth speaker', labeled 'FREE SPEECH'.
pages.page_summary: This page from Topolski's Chronicle features a series of expressive ink sketches illustrating key social and institutional themes such as parliament, learning, law, church, leisure, finance, and public speaking/free speech.
